Abu Hurairah .% reported that the Prophet £€ said, “Indeed, this fire of yours is one of seventy parts from the Fire of Hell. And it (the fire of this world) was struck by the sea twice, and were it not for that, Allah would not have made benefit therein for anyone.” (Ahmad) This hadith fulfills the conditions of Bukhaaree and Muslim.

Abu Hurairah & said, “I heard Abu Al-Qaasim (Muhammad 2s) say, “The fire of the son of Adam, which you ignite, is one of seventy parts from the Fire of Hell.” (Ahmad)

516 Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations

#6 said, “The Fire of Hell surpasses it by 69 parts, each of which is equal to the heat of the fire of this world.” (Ahmad)

Another Chain With Another Wording

Abu Hurairah 2s reported that the Messenger of Allah # said, “This Fire is one of one hundred parts from the Fire of Hell.” (Ahmad) The chain of this narration fulfills the conditions of Muslim, but in its wording there is Gharaabah, for most narrations from Abu Hurairah 4 mention “one of seventy parts.” The hadith has been related with that same wording by someone else — by way of ‘Abdullah ibn Mas’ood +,

‘Abdullah .t: reported that the Messenger of Allah #¢ said, “The good dream is a glad tiding, and it is one of seventy parts of Prophethood. And this fire of yours is ww of seventy parts frum the poisons of the Hellfire. And as long as the slave (of Allah) is waiting for the prayer, he is in (the state of) Prayer, so long as he does not do Hadath (something t “5 out of the state of purity, such as passing wind).” Al-L.. .. ar related it and said, “It has been related Mawgoofan (a saying, nui of the Prophet #%, but of a Companion) by way of Abu Sa’eed #),”

Abu Hurairha 2s related that the Messenger of Allah #8 said, “Do you know what the example of this fire of yours is compared to the Fire of Hell? It has 70 times more smoke than the smoke of this fire of yours.” Al-Haafiz Ad-Diyaa said, “Ibn Mus’ab related it from Maalik, who deemed it to be Mmwqoof (not a saying of the Prophet # but of a Companion). With me, it fulfills the conditions of As-Sahech.

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations S17

The Hellfire Was Ignited For 3000 Years Until It Became Black And Dark

The Heat Of The Hellfire Never Goes Out And One Does Nat Warm Oneself By Its Flame (i.c., One Does Not Find Comfort From It, As Opposed To The Fire Of This World: If One Is Not Touching Fire On A Cold Day, But Is Near It, One Finds Comfort And Warmth From It)

Salmaan 4 related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “The heat of the Hellfire does not go out, nor is warmness (the comforting kind) achieved by its flame.” He # then recited:

“And We shall say: “Taste you the torment of the burning (Firey This narration is related by Al-Haafidh Al-Baihagee, who said, “To ascribe it to the Prophet #% is weak.” He then related it through

another chain Mazwqoofan (as a saying, not of the Prophet 28, but of a Companion)

In a narration related by Ibn Mirdawai, Anas % reported that the Messenger of Allah x recited this saying of Allah #:

G ehewe ope A

Bhs al Ga Gyles TB ye

518 Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations

“O you who believe! Ward off yourselves and your families against

a Fire (Hell) whose fuel is men and stones, over which are

(appointed) angels stern (and) severe, who disobey not (from

executing) the commands they receive from Allah ¥é, but do that

Book of The End: Great Trials and Tribulations 319

said, “This hadith is Hasan, and its chain is good.”

On The Day Of Resurrection, Abu Taalib Will Incur The Smallest Degree Of Punishment From The Dwellers Of Hell

Abu Sa’eed Al-Khudree related the uncle of the Messenger of Allah #%, Abu Taalib, was mentioned in his presence. Then the Messenger of Allah #% said, “Perhaps my intercession will benefit him on the Day of Resurrection, so that he is placed in a shallow (part of the Hellfire), where (the Fire) will reach his ankles, and from that his brain will boil.” (Bukhaaree) In the narration of Muslim, Abu Sa’eed reported that the Messenger of Allah # said, “The dweller of the Hellfire who will be punished the least is he who will wear shoes of Fire: his brain will boil from the heat of his shoes.”

Abu Sa’ced xeported that the Messenger of Allah 2g said, “The least punished of the dwellers of Hell is he who has two shoes on his feet: his brain will boil from them.” Ahmad related this narration, and then he mentioned the completion of the hadith. An-No’maan 4 related that he heard the Prophet #% say, “Indeed, the east punished from the dwellers of Hell on the Day of Resurrection is a man, for whom live coals are placed under his feet: from them his brain will boil.” (Bukhaaree) Muslim related it from the hadith of Sho’bah.

An-No’maan ibn Basheer related that he heard the Prophet #% say, “On the Day of Resurrection, the least punished of the dwellers of Hell is a man, for whom two live coals are placed on the soles of his feet. From them his brain will boil just as a caldron boils and a Quinqum (a kind of boiler made of copper) boils.” (Bukhaarec)

Ibn ‘Abbaas .& related that the Messenger of Allah #% said, “The least punished from the dwellers of Hell is Abu Taalib; he will wear two shoes, from which his brain will boil.” (Muslim)

520 Book of The End : Great Trials and Tribulations

Abu Hurairah # related that the Prophet 2% said, “The least punished of the dwellers of Hell will have upon him two shoes, from which his brain will boil.” (Ahmad) In a narration having the same chain of natrators, the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “Had you known what I know, you would have laughed only a little and you would have cried much.”

Anas 4s related that the Messenger of Allah 2% said, “By the One Who has the soul of Muhammad in His Hand, had you seen what I saw, you would have cried much, and you would have laughed only a little” They said, “O Messenger of Allah, and what did you see?” He # said, “I saw Paradise and Hell.” (Ahmad)
